sites.message_template: CUDA driver version unavailable; allowing flash attention based on compute capability
SAMEPORT mode enabled (gRPC shares listener with REST via cmux/protocol).\n
Config: REST={}  gRPC=http://{}  model={}  (post-build quality parity + edge cases)\n\n
gRPC cancel test (mid-stream): cleanly_stopped={} chunks_before_cancel={} partial_prompt_tokens={} partial_completion_tokens={}\n
\n\n=== COMPARATIVE METRICS TABLE (side-by-side REST vs gRPC; expect token parity) ===
\n\n=== DETAILED TECHNICAL COMPARATIVE ANALYSIS REPORT (REAL gRPC DATA + EDGES) ===
- Side-by-side token/metrics parity for transport validation (post-fix).
- Mid-gen stream cancellation + token counts (simple_user_prompt cancel subcase).
- OTEL/metrics/pprof exposure check (if server surfaces them; otelconnect spans in logs/collector).
- SAMEPORT + separate port support via flags/env.
- Runnable post `make local` / build-local.sh + `./ollama serve` (with OLLAMA_GRPC_*).
{} stream={}: in={} out={} tot={}ms tps={} ttft={}ms wreq={} wresp={} done={} tools={} struct={} cancel={} chunks={}\n
{} non-stream: prompt_tokens_identical={} (REST={} gRPC={}), wire_delta_req={} bytes (gRPC typically compact for tools)\n

\nContext overhead note: Server-side prompt_eval_count / eval_count are identical (or near) because message normalization + inference happens after the protocol adapter in the shared core (*Server + scheduler + llama). gRPC (protobuf + Connect) compactness reduces client-side serialization cost and wire bytes vs JSON, giving app-layer headroom for richer agent state (tools, history, vision).

\nEfficiency: Compute side (tokens, TPS, runner llama-server) is shared/identical. Differences are only in wire framing (proto vs json), client overhead, and transport (h2c flow control + backpressure in gRPC streams).

\nUse cases: REST for compatibility/debug/curl/OpenAI SDKs; gRPC for typed, cancellable, efficient agentic flows (tools in-stream), better observability (OTEL spans via otelconnect, rich status), backpressure, and microservice clients.

\nAdditional metrics captured: TTFT (time to first token, critical for UX), cancellation responsiveness (prevents wasted GPU cycles on mid-gen client disconnects; see cancel subtest with partial token counts), tool fidelity (shared converters ensure same tool_calls count), structured validity (json format roundtrips), multi-turn growth, client retry paths, server health (gRPC listener, OTEL, runner discovery via shared sched).
\nCancellation edge summary: {} runs; clean stops reported in gRPC mid-stream (tokens partial but stop respected; expect no further generation post-cancel).\n
\n=== Observability / OTEL / Metrics / Edge Check (if exposed) ===
-> goroutine count sample: {} (use during concurrent gRPC+REST load to check for leaks vs baseline)\n
OTEL note: Spans/metrics for gRPC (and shared paths) come from otelconnect interceptors + OTel SDK (if OTEL_TRACES_EXPORTER/OTEL_METRICS_EXPORTER or equiv configured in env).
Look in server logs for 'component=grpc' + reason + stream_id + tokens + duration_ms (rich slog from reliable overlay).
For full spans: run with OTEL exporter (e.g. to collector or console) and/or inspect pprof while running quality cases.
gRPC-specific: health/reflection (if enabled) also aid discovery; use grpcurl or buf curl for live surface.
